skill.name: medical-translation
skill.description: Provides precise translation of medical terminology between Chinese and English for academic writing workflows, using a verified in-memory dictionary with structured output.
skill.summary: Accurate medical terminology translation for Chinese-English academic writing with structured, reproducible output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 46 patterns across 5 files, including backtick-based command execution, path traversal, system reconnaissance, hardcoded URLs, and weak cryptography. After semantic evaluation, all 46 findings are confirmed false positives: backtick detections are markdown inline code formatting, path traversal hits appear in a security prevention checklist, cryptography flags target YAML frontmatter text with no cryptographic content, and system reconnaissance hits are benign documentation phrases. The skill contains only in-memory dictionary lookups with no network access, no file system writes beyond standard library operations, and no external command execution. No prompt injection attempts were found.

content.value_statement: Medical translation errors can cause serious misunderstandings in research and clinical communication. This skill provides precise medical terminology translation across languages using a structured, evidence-based workflow with clear assumptions and reproducible output.
actual_capabilities: Translate medical terms between Chinese and English using a verified terminology dictionary
Provide structured translation output with source language, target language, and translation notes
Flag untranslated terms for manual review when they fall outside the known dictionary
Execute deterministic smoke tests to verify workspace readiness before translation
Return clear error messages when required inputs are missing or language pairs are unsupported
limitations: Only supports Chinese-English and English-Chinese medical term translation currently
Cannot translate full medical documents; designed for individual terms and short paragraphs
Translation dictionary covers approximately 10 common medical terms out of the box
Does not connect to external medical databases or APIs for term verification

use_cases.title: Medical Researcher Translating Key Terms
use_cases.description: A medical researcher needs to accurately translate key terminology between Chinese and English for a bilingual academic paper.

use_cases.title: Clinical Professional Verifying Translations
use_cases.description: A clinician needs to confirm the correct translation of medical conditions when communicating with international colleagues.

use_cases.title: Medical Student Learning Terminology
use_cases.description: A medical student learning in a second language needs quick, reliable translations of common medical terms during study.

prompt_templates.title: Translate a Single Medical Term
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the medical term 'Acute Myeloid Leukemia' from English to Chinese. Return the translation with any relevant notes about the term.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic single-term translation with no additional context

prompt_templates.title: Translate with Medical Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the term 'hypertension' from English to Chinese. Context: This appears in a cardiology research paper about blood pressure management in elderly patients.
prompt_templates.scenario: Translation with clinical context to improve accuracy

prompt_templates.title: Batch Medical Term Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the following medical terms from Chinese to English: 急性髓系白血病, 高血压, 糖尿病, 心肌梗死. For each term, provide the translation and note if it requires manual review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multiple terms in a single translation request

prompt_templates.title: Validate Workspace and Run Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: First run the smoke test to verify the medical-translation workspace is ready. Then translate 'cerebral infarction' from English to Chinese. Report the smoke test results and the translation together.

output_examples.input: Translate 'Acute Myeloid Leukemia' from English to Chinese
output_examples.output: The translation returns '急性髓系白血病 (AML)' with the original term, source and target languages, and an empty notes field since the term was found in the standard dictionary.

output_examples.input: Translate '糖尿病' from Chinese to English
output_examples.output: The translation returns 'Diabetes Mellitus' with metadata showing Chinese as source and English as target. No review notes are added since the term exists in the dictionary.

output_examples.input: Translate 'pulmonary embolism' from English to Chinese
output_examples.output: The translation returns '[Requires manual confirmation] pulmonary embolism' with a note stating the term is not in the standard dictionary and requires expert review.

best_practices: Always specify both source and target languages explicitly in every translation request
Provide medical context when available to help the translator select the most accurate terminology
Review flagged translations that require manual confirmation before using them in clinical or publication settings
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for translating full medical documents, patient records, or clinical trial data
Do not assume translations are clinically verified without review by a domain expert
Do not skip the source and target language parameters when submitting a translation request

faq.question: What language pairs does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill currently supports Chinese-English and English-Chinese medical terminology translation.

faq.question: Can it translate full medical research papers?
faq.answer: No, it is designed for individual medical terms and short paragraphs, not full documents.

faq.question: How accurate are the translations?
faq.answer: Translations use a verified medical terminology dictionary, but all results should be reviewed by a domain expert before clinical or publication use.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No, all translations use an in-memory dictionary with no external API calls or network requests.

faq.question: Can I add custom medical terms to the dictionary?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit the dictionary in scripts/main.py to add new terms and their translations.

faq.question: What happens if a term is not found in the dictionary?
faq.answer: The skill flags the term for manual review and returns the original text with a note recommending expert verification.
